codeReview.summary: Clean, focused implementation of F1-based context switching between text editor and image viewer. The compositor correctly intercepts F1 key events before forwarding to the editor, toggles IMAGE_MODE, re-renders the content surface for the new mode, and blocks keyboard input from reaching the editor in image mode. Boot now starts in editor mode (previously image mode). Status bar updates correctly to show image dimensions or char count depending on mode. 8 well-structured tests validate deterministic rendering, cursor preservation, key mapping safety, and chrome persistence. QEMU visual verification confirms the full demo flow (type → switch → view image → switch back → text preserved).

issues.description: Redundant content surface re-render when switching from image mode back to editor. The F1 handler calls render_content_surface() and sets changed=true. Then in the event loop's 'if changed' block, render_content_surface() is called a second time because 'changed && !in_image_mode' is true. The second render is idempotent (deterministic output) so this is not a bug, but it wastes one full content surface render per editor→image→editor round-trip. A simple fix would be to set a 'content_already_rendered' flag in the F1 handler or skip content re-render when the mode just changed.

The context_switch_f1_keycode_not_printable test embeds a copy of the input driver's keycode-to-ASCII map (MAP: [u8; 58]). If the input driver's actual map changes (e.g., adding more keycodes or modifying the mapping), this test copy will become stale and won't detect the drift. Consider importing the map from a shared location or adding a comment cross-referencing the canonical map location.

The context_switch_status_bar_content_differs test manually constructs status bar text strings rather than exercising the actual render_status_bar function. This means format string changes in the compositor won't be caught by this test. Acceptable given the no_std architecture constraint (compositor code can't be imported into host tests), but worth noting as a coverage gap.

sharedStateObservations.observation: architecture.md describes the display pipeline but doesn't mention context switching between content modes (editor/image viewer). Now that F1 toggles modes, the architecture doc could document this capability and the IMAGE_MODE state in the compositor. The compositor's role expanded from 'sole writer to document state' to also 'content mode manager'.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md — Display Pipeline section describes 4 processes but doesn't mention multi-mode content rendering. Compositor now manages IMAGE_MODE global state toggled by F1.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The test for F1 keycode safety (context_switch_f1_keycode_not_printable) copies the input driver's keycode-to-ASCII map into the test file. This creates a maintenance risk if the map changes. The codebase doesn't have a pattern for sharing constants between no_std binaries and host test crates — this may warrant a shared constants approach or at minimum documenting the cross-reference.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: system/test/tests/drawing.rs:3839 — MAP: [u8; 58] is a copy of the mapping from the input driver. The canonical map lives in system/services/drivers/virtio-input/main.rs.

summary: Context switching feature passes review. Implementation is clean and minimal: 52 new lines in the compositor handle F1 key interception, IMAGE_MODE toggling, mode-appropriate content surface rendering, and input blocking in image mode. 8 tests (399 lines) validate the drawing library's deterministic rendering guarantees that underpin context switching. Three non-blocking issues noted: (1) redundant content surface render on image→editor switch, (2) duplicated keycode map in test, (3) status bar test uses manually constructed strings. All are minor and don't affect correctness. Visual verification via QEMU confirms the full demo flow works end-to-end.
